leaders.name: Sato Eisaku
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Railways official, served in multiple cabinet posts before becoming PM. Nobel Peace Prize laureate (1974) for non-nuclear principles.
key_economic_events: Nixon Shock (1971) - end of dollar-gold convertibility
Japan surpasses West Germany as 2nd largest economy

leaders.name: Tanaka Kakuei
leaders.background_detail: Self-made construction magnate with no university degree. Known as 'the computerized bulldozer'. Architect of Japan's infrastructure boom.
key_economic_events: 1973 Oil Crisis - devastating impact on oil-dependent Japan
Japanese Archipelago Remodeling Plan - massive infrastructure spending

leaders.name: Miki Takeo
leaders.background_detail: Known as 'Mr. Clean' for anti-corruption stance. Long-serving parliamentarian focused on political reform.

leaders.name: Fukuda Takeo
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official, specialist in fiscal policy. Father of later PM Fukuda Yasuo.
key_economic_events: Fukuda Doctrine - expanded relations with ASEAN
Fiscal stimulus to combat post-oil-crisis stagnation

leaders.name: Ohira Masayoshi
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official and Foreign Minister. Died in office during 1980 election campaign.
key_economic_events: Proposed consumption tax (failed, contributed to political crisis)

leaders.name: Suzuki Zenko
leaders.background_detail: Fisheries policy specialist, considered a compromise candidate after Ohira's sudden death.

leaders.name: Nakasone Yasuhiro
leaders.background_detail: Former Home Ministry official, hawkish defense stance, close relationship with Reagan and Thatcher. Pursued privatization and deregulation.
key_economic_events: Privatization of JNR (Japan National Railways) into JR Group

leaders.name: Takeshita Noboru
leaders.background_detail: Tanaka faction heir, master of backroom politics. Successfully introduced the consumption tax.

leaders.name: Uno Sosuke
leaders.background_detail: Former Foreign Minister, one of the shortest-serving PMs. Resigned after personal scandal.
key_economic_events: Geisha scandal forced resignation after 69 days
LDP suffered major upper house election defeat

leaders.name: Kaifu Toshiki
leaders.background_detail: Clean-image politician chosen to restore LDP credibility after scandals. Relatively weak factional power base.

leaders.name: Miyazawa Kiichi
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official, fluent English speaker, experienced finance minister. Last PM of unbroken LDP rule.
key_economic_events: LDP loses power for first time (1993)

leaders.name: Hosokawa Morihiro
leaders.background_detail: Former governor of Kumamoto Prefecture, descended from feudal lords. Led the first non-LDP coalition government since 1955.
key_economic_events: Electoral reform - new mixed electoral system

leaders.name: Hata Tsutomu
leaders.background_detail: Former LDP member who broke away. Served only 64 days as PM, shortest post-war tenure at the time.

leaders.name: Murayama Tomiichi
leaders.background_detail: First socialist PM since 1948. Led unlikely coalition with LDP. Known for Murayama Statement on WWII apology.
key_economic_events: Jusen housing loan crisis - first bailout

leaders.name: Hashimoto Ryutaro
leaders.background_detail: Known for administrative reform agenda. Raised consumption tax from 3% to 5%, contributing to recession.
key_economic_events: Yamaichi Securities and Hokkaido Takushoku Bank collapse

leaders.name: Obuchi Keizo
leaders.background_detail: Initially derided as 'cold pizza', proved more capable than expected. Died of stroke while in office.
key_economic_events: Financial Revitalization Law - bad loan resolution
Suffered stroke in office, died May 2000

leaders.name: Mori Yoshiro
leaders.background_detail: Succeeded Obuchi after backroom deal. Historically low approval ratings. Later led Tokyo 2020 Olympics organizing committee.

leaders.name: Koizumi Junichiro
leaders.background_detail: Maverick reformer who broke LDP conventions. Privatized postal system, pursued structural reform. Hugely popular 'lion-maned' PM.
key_economic_events: Structural reform - 'reform with no sacred cows'
Japan's longest postwar economic expansion began
Quantitative easing by BoJ during tenure
Yasukuni Shrine visits strained China/Korea relations

leaders.name: Abe Shinzo
leaders.background_detail: Youngest postwar PM at the time. First term cut short by health issues and scandals. Grandson of PM Kishi Nobusuke.
key_economic_events: Cabinet scandals and pension record crisis

leaders.name: Fukuda Yasuo
leaders.background_detail: Son of former PM Fukuda Takeo. Former oil company executive before entering politics. Steady but uninspiring leadership.
key_economic_events: Twisted Diet - opposition controlled upper house

leaders.name: Aso Taro
leaders.background_detail: Grandson of PM Yoshida Shigeru. Cement company executive. Known for manga enthusiasm and verbal gaffes.
key_economic_events: Global Financial Crisis - Lehman shock hit Japan hard
LDP suffered historic election defeat to DPJ

leaders.name: Hatoyama Yukio
leaders.background_detail: Stanford PhD in engineering. Heir to Bridgestone fortune. Led DPJ to historic victory ending LDP rule.
key_economic_events: DPJ first government - promised to shift from bureaucrat-led to politician-led governance
Child allowance and highway toll-free policies
Resigned over broken Okinawa base promise

leaders.name: Kan Naoto
leaders.background_detail: Grassroots activist turned politician. Patent attorney background. Led Japan through triple disaster.
key_economic_events: Great East Japan Earthquake and Tsunami (2011-03-11)
Yen hit postwar record high of 75.35

leaders.name: Noda Yoshihiko
leaders.background_detail: Former finance minister. Pushed through controversial consumption tax hike legislation. Last DPJ prime minister.
key_economic_events: Consumption tax increase legislation passed (to 8% then 10%)

leaders.name: Abe Shinzo
leaders.background_detail: Longest-serving PM in Japanese history. Architect of 'Abenomics' - three arrows of monetary easing, fiscal stimulus, structural reform. Assassinated July 2022.
key_economic_events: BoJ massive quantitative and qualitative easing (QQE)
Resigned due to ulcerative colitis recurrence

leaders.name: Suga Yoshihide
leaders.background_detail: Former Chief Cabinet Secretary under Abe for nearly 8 years. Son of a strawberry farmer from Akita. Focused on digital reform and COVID vaccination.
key_economic_events: Declined to run for LDP leadership reelection

leaders.name: Kishida Fumio
leaders.background_detail: Former Foreign Minister, Hiroshima native. Pursued 'new capitalism' blending growth and distribution. Stepped down amid LDP fundraising scandal.

leaders.name: Ishiba Shigeru
leaders.background_detail: Former Defense Minister, known as military policy expert and LDP intra-party critic. Won LDP leadership on 5th attempt after decades as a maverick backbencher. Resigned September 7, 2025 after LDP-Komeito coalition lost majority in House of Councillors.
key_economic_events: LDP lost majority in 2024 lower house election - minority government
US-Japan trade deal framework negotiations (15% tariff, $550B investment)
Resigned September 7, 2025 after upper house coalition defeat

leaders.name: Takaichi Sanae
leaders.background_detail: Japan's first female Prime Minister. Ultraconservative with ties to far-right LDP factions. Former Minister of Internal Affairs and Communications. Close ideological successor to Abe Shinzo. Known for Yasukuni Shrine visits and hawkish defense posture. Won LDP presidential election 237-149 in lower house vote, elected by parliament October 21, 2025.
key_economic_events: US-Japan Trade Deal finalized July 2025: 15% tariff rate, $550B investment commitment
BoJ rate normalization continued (0.75% by December 2025)
Abenomics continuation and expansion — fiscal stimulus, defense spending increase
Yen remained soft at 156-157/USD despite BOJ hikes

central_bank_governor.title: Governor of the Bank of Japan

leaders.name: Sasaki Tadashi
leaders.background_detail: Career Bank of Japan official. Navigated the Nixon Shock and first oil crisis.
key_economic_events: Nixon Shock - end of Bretton Woods (1971)
First Oil Crisis (1973) - inflation surged past 20%
Tight monetary policy to combat inflation

leaders.name: Morinaga Teiichiro
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official. Managed post-oil crisis monetary stabilization.
key_economic_events: Yen appreciation after Smithsonian Agreement collapse

leaders.name: Maekawa Haruo
leaders.background_detail: Career BoJ official. Later authored the 'Maekawa Report' (1986) recommending economic restructuring.
key_economic_events: Tight monetary policy brought inflation under control

leaders.name: Sumita Satoshi
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance Vice Minister. Oversaw aggressive monetary easing post-Plaza Accord that fueled the asset bubble.
key_economic_events: Aggressive monetary easing to offset yen impact
Asset bubble inflated - land and stock prices soared

leaders.name: Mieno Yasushi
leaders.background_detail: Career BoJ official known as 'the Demon' for aggressive rate hikes. Deliberately burst the asset bubble.
key_economic_events: Raised discount rate 5 times to burst bubble (2.5% → 6%)
Began cutting rates as recession deepened

leaders.name: Matsushita Yasuo
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official. Oversaw period of deepening financial crisis and banking failures.
key_economic_events: Discount rate cut to historic low of 0.5% (1995)
BoJ scandal led to early departure

leaders.name: Hayami Masaru
leaders.background_detail: Former Nissho Iwai (trading company) chairman. BoJ career before business. Advocated BoJ independence from government.
key_economic_events: Premature rate hike (2000) reversed after criticism

leaders.name: Fukui Toshihiko
leaders.background_detail: Career BoJ official. Expanded then exited QE. Personal investment scandal (Murakami Fund) damaged credibility.
key_economic_events: Global financial crisis emerging at term end

leaders.name: Shirakawa Masaaki
leaders.background_detail: BoJ career official with academic background (University of Chicago). Cautious monetary policy drew criticism for insufficient action against deflation.
key_economic_events: Great East Japan Earthquake emergency liquidity (2011)
Resigned early as Abe government pressured for change

leaders.name: Kuroda Haruhiko
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Ministry of Finance / Asian Development Bank
leaders.background_detail: Former Ministry of Finance official and ADB President. Architect of unprecedented monetary easing under Abenomics. Served two full terms.
key_economic_events: 2% inflation target with 'whatever it takes' commitment
BoJ became largest holder of Japanese stocks via ETFs
Yen depreciation accelerated in final year
Maintained ultra-loose policy despite global tightening

leaders.name: Ueda Kazuo
leaders.background_detail: MIT-trained economist, former BoJ Policy Board member, University of Tokyo professor. First academic governor since WWII.
key_economic_events: August 2024 market shock after rate hike
